title: Deskpro vs Freshdesk for Power users
meta_description: See why Freshdesk fails first when the system cannot be self-hosted and requires SaaS infrastructure and why Deskpro is the better pick for power users.
constraint_lens: You need a support tool that can adapt to your infrastructure requirements without limiting how or where it is deployed.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for power users who need full control over hosting through self-hosted or on-premise deployment.
one_second_verdict.reason: Freshdesk fails first because it requires SaaS infrastructure and cannot be deployed in a self-hosted environment.

verdict.summary: Deskpro is the better choice when your priority is controlling where and how your support system is hosted. It offers self-hosted and on-premise deployment options, allowing full control over data and infrastructure. Freshdesk is a SaaS-only platform, which simplifies setup but limits flexibility for teams that require ownership of their hosting environment.
verdict.decision_rule: If the system cannot be self-hosted and requires SaaS infrastructure, Freshdesk fails first.

sections.heading: Why Deskpro fits this situation
sections.content: This setup fits a power user who needs to control infrastructure decisions, including where data is stored and how the system is managed. SaaS tools remove that control and can create limitations. Deskpro supports self-hosting, making it suitable for environments where flexibility and ownership are required.

bullets.point: Deskpro can be deployed on your own servers or infrastructure instead of relying on a vendor-hosted system.
bullets.why_it_matters: You control data storage, access, and system behavior without being tied to external infrastructure.

bullets.point: System updates and configurations can be managed internally based on your own requirements.
bullets.why_it_matters: This allows you to customize and evolve the system without waiting on vendor limitations.

bullets.point: Data remains within your environment rather than being stored in a third-party SaaS platform.
bullets.why_it_matters: This supports compliance, security, and control needs that grow with more advanced setups.

bullets.point: Freshdesk is fully hosted as a SaaS platform with no infrastructure setup required.
bullets.why_it_matters: This makes it easy to start quickly, but removes control over hosting and deployment.

bullets.point: Updates and maintenance are handled automatically by the vendor.
bullets.why_it_matters: This reduces operational effort, but limits customization of system behavior.

bullets.point: The platform is optimized for cloud-based access across teams without managing servers.
bullets.why_it_matters: This simplifies usage, but does not meet needs where infrastructure control is required.

sections.heading: How each tool can break down

items.fails_when: Deskpro starts to break when a team does not have the resources or expertise to manage its own hosting and infrastructure.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Freshdesk if you want a fully managed system without handling servers or deployment.

items.fails_when: Freshdesk starts to break when the team requires full control over hosting, data storage, or deployment environment.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Deskpro when self-hosting and infrastructure control are necessary.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: This verdict might flip if your team does not need control over hosting and prefers a system that is ready to use without managing infrastructure. In that case, Freshdesk’s SaaS model may be more practical.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Pick Deskpro if you need self-hosted or on-premise deployment.
Pick Freshdesk if you want a fully managed SaaS tool with no infrastructure work.
If controlling hosting matters, choose Deskpro.

faqs.q: Why does Deskpro win for power users?
faqs.a: Because it allows self-hosting and full control over infrastructure, which is critical for advanced setups.
No, Freshdesk is a SaaS-only platform and cannot be deployed on your own servers.

faqs.q: When should I choose Freshdesk instead?
faqs.a: Choose it when you prefer a fully managed system without handling infrastructure or hosting.

faqs.q: What is the main difference between these tools?
faqs.a: Deskpro supports self-hosting and on-premise deployment, while Freshdesk requires SaaS infrastructure.
